dimensions.good: Directly completes the requested task or produces the requested artifact/check.
dimensions.bad: Answers around the task, misses one required artifact, or stops at advice.
dimensions.unacceptable: Does not do the requested work or replaces execution with generic commentary.

dimensions.good: Claims are tied to repo files, verification artifacts, or explicit checks.
dimensions.bad: Some claims are plausible but not clearly grounded.
dimensions.unacceptable: Invents files, checks, platform proof, or shipped features.

dimensions.good: Separates facts, inferences, assumptions, and missing proof; prefers a truthful range over fake precision.
dimensions.bad: Uncertainty is mentioned but not connected to the decision or estimate.

dimensions.good: Keeps local-first, proof-first sequencing and resists scope jumps or vanity work.
dimensions.bad: Mentions priorities but still drifts into secondary scope.
dimensions.unacceptable: Promotes cloud/UI/feature-cloning ahead of unresolved runtime correctness.

dimensions.good: Names the next reversible step, blockers, and concrete verification needed.
dimensions.bad: Next steps are generic or not ordered.
dimensions.unacceptable: No practical next move is given when one is available.

metrics.description: Fraction of cases that pass required binary gates and reach an acceptable rubric floor.
Fraction of cases containing a material unsupported claim or fabricated proof signal.
How often the answer explicitly marks unverified or blocked areas instead of guessing.
Optional for model/tool loops; measure only when the eval runner invokes real inference or tools.
Optional for model/tool loops; track only when inference/tool cost is material.

judgementPolicy.pairwiseTiebreaker: Use pairwise comparison only after binary gates and rubric floors are satisfied.

humanCalibration.sampleRate: At least 10% of daily cases and every held-out case on release candidates.
humanCalibration.disagreementRule: Two reviewers calibrate rubric drift before changing prompts, tools, or thresholds.
